channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korea's Yoon replaces defense chief as parliament is poised to vote on his impeachment
item.description: South Korea’s president has accepted the resignation offer by his defense minister, as opposition parties moved to impeach both men over the stunning yet short-lived imposition of martial law that brought armed troops into Seoul streets.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim And Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korean president's martial law declaration a 'blunder,' could embolden North Korea, expert says
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law and now faces impeachment over the surprising move from opposition lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 6 hours of anger, bravery and defiance during martial law in South Korea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol shocked the nation when he declared martial law and called in troops to secure the National Assembly building where parliament sits.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Takeaways from Biden's Africa trip: Pardon of son Hunter overshadows official business
item.description: President Joe Biden’s long-delayed trip to Africa was overshadowed by his controversial pardon of his son Hunter.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mexico seizes record amount of fentanyl days after Trump issued tariff threats
item.description: Mexican troops seized a record amount of fentanyl pills in the state of Sinaloa on Tuesday, which broke a record, authorities said this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A major power plant fails in Cuba, plunging the island into darkness — again
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — A new, widespread power outage plunged Cuba into darkness on Wednesday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, leaving millions without electricity and forcing authorities to suspend classes and work activities indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump appoints former Middle East negotiator as hostage affairs envoy
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced his selection of Adam Boehler to serve as special presidential envoy for hostage affairs in his second term. While the position holds the rank of an ambassador, it does not require Senate confirmation.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Romania's surprise front-runner says he's not pro-Russia. But Romania alleges he had Moscow's help
item.description: IZVORANI, Romania (AP) — The surprise front-runner in Romania’s presidential election insisted to The Associated Press in an interview Wednesday that he is not pro-Russian — hours before Romania declassified intelligence alleging Moscow ran an online campaign to promote him to voters.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH and VADIM GHIRDA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korean President Yoon's martial law declaration raises questions over his political future
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — President Yoon Suk Yeol’s stunning martial law declaration lasted just hours, but experts say it raised serious questions about his ability to govern for the remaining 2 1/2 years of his term and whether he will abide by democratic principles.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas issues instructions to 'neutralize' hostages if Israel attempts rescue operation
item.description: Hamas threatened to "neutralize" its hostages if Israel attempted another rescue operation, according to an internal statement seen by Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Archaeologists in Denmark discover over 100 weapons from the Iron Age during excavations
item.description: Archaeologists in Denmark unearthed what they've suggested is a sacrifice of weapons. The weapons likely belonged to a powerful chieftain thousands of years ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea opposition parties move to impeach president over martial law declaration
item.description: Opposition parties moved to impeach South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol on Wednesday over his sudden declaration of martial law, which thrust one of the U.S.'s closest allies into peril.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: What to know about South Korea's short-lived and chaotic period of martial law
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — South Korea's President Yoon Suk Yeol faced parliamentary moves to impeach him after he sent heavily armed forces into Seoul's streets with his baffling and sudden declaration of martial law that harkened to the country’s past dictatorships.
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Activists file second impeachment complaint against embattled Philippine Vice President Duterte
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A group led by left-wing activists filed a second impeachment complaint against the Philippine vice president on Wednesday over her alleged misuse of government funds and demanded that she be permanently barred from holding public office.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japanese court convicts Australian who says she was tricked into smuggling drugs
item.description: CHIBA, Japan (AP) — A Japanese court on Wednesday sentenced an Australian woman to six years in prison for smuggling amphetamines into the country, despite accepting her testimony that she was tricked as part of an online romance scam.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine ceasefire terms come into focus as Trump returns
item.description: Potential paths to a ceasefire in Ukraine are coming into focus as President-elect Trump approaches Inauguration Day, with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky signaling his concessions to end the war largely along the current front lines in the east.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: French lawmakers vote to oust prime minister in the first successful no-confidence vote since 1962
item.description: France’s far-right and left-wing lawmakers have joined together to vote on a no-confidence motion prompted by budget disputes that forces Prime Minister Michel Barnier and his Cabinet to resign.
item.dc:creator: Tom Nouvian And Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An Israeli strike kills a person in Lebanon, further shaking the tenuous ceasefire
item.description: NABATIYEH, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli forces carried out several new drone and artillery strikes in Lebanon on Tuesday, including a deadly strike that the Health Ministry and state media said killed one person, further shaking a tenuous ceasefire meant to end more than a year of fighting with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: BASSAM HATOUM and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shock martial law edict in South Korea follows chaotic recent history: A look at the crucial context
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — It may seem that the wild predawn scenes in Seoul, with hundreds of armed troops and frantic lawmakers storming South Korea's parliament building after the president suddenly declared martial law, came out of nowhere.
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippines says China's coast guard fired water cannons and sideswiped its patrol vessel
item.description: Philippine officials say Chinese coast guard vessels backed by navy ships have fired powerful water cannons and blocked and sideswiped a Philippine patrol vessel at a disputed shoal in the South China Sea.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Namibia will have its first female leader after VP wins presidential election for the ruling party
item.description: WINDHOEK, Namibia (AP) — Namibia elected its first female leader as Vice President Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah was declared the winner Tuesday of a presidential election last week that was tarnished by technical glitches that caused a three-day extension to allow votes to be cast, and rejected as illegal
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US 'seriously concerned' by developments in South Korea
item.description: The United States was surprised by South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol’s brief declaration of emergency martial law.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu reacts after Trump warns of 'hell to pay' if Hamas doesn't free hostages
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says President-elect Trump has the emphasis in "the right place" with his latest demand for Hamas to release hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean parliament votes to defy president by lifting his declaration of martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law late Tuesday, vowing to eliminate “anti-state” forces as he struggles against an opposition that controls the country’s parliament and that he accuses of sympathizing with communist North Korea.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US blasts countries that ‘stood in the way of progress to protect their profits’ over collapsed plastic talks
item.description: The White House criticized a group of oil-rich countries as well as corporations who make money from plastic production in a new statement after global talks for a treaty to reduce plastic waste collapsed.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 11 in Lebanon after fire exchanges with Hezbollah that test ceasefire’s limits
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel unleashed its largest wave of airstrikes across Lebanon since agreeing to a ceasefire with Hezbollah last week, killing at least 11 people on Monday after the Lebanese militant group fired a volley of projectiles as a warning over what it said were Israeli truce violations.
item.dc:creator: NATALIE MELTZER and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: IDF soldiers accuse UN peacekeepers of enabling Hezbollah terrorists amid increasing cease-fire violations
item.description: In southern Lebanon, Israeli forces confront entrenched Hezbollah positions and question UNIFIL’s ability to enforce its mandate even before the cease-fire agreement was drawn up.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hong Kong launches panda sculpture tour as the city hopes the bear craze boosts tourism
item.description: Thousands of giant panda sculptures will greet residents and tourists starting Saturday in Hong Kong, where enthusiasm for the bears has grown since two cubs were born in a local theme park.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, WFLA, NBC

item.title: American-Israeli IDF platoon commander killed in battle, body held in Gaza, IDF says
item.description: A 21-year-old American-Israeli believed to have been held hostage since October 2023 was confirmed dead by the Israel Defense Forces on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas releases new hostage video of American-Israeli Edan Alexander
item.description: Hamas released a propaganda video Saturday showing the 20-year-old Alexander alive, marking his first proof of life since being kidnapped.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Nigeria confirms that at least 27 people died and more than 100 are missing after boat capsized
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — At least 27 people died and more than 100, mostly women, were missing on Friday, after a boat transporting them to a food market capsized along the River Niger in northern Nigeria, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Powerful Mexican cartel accused of forced labor, human trafficking
item.description: The Gulf Cartel is a long-standing, powerful drug trafficking organization that operates throughout Tamaulipas.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation
